codeReview.summary: The feature adds typed Specta-backed prewarm/discard commands and removes automatic bootstrap timing dependence, but the backend never ties the prepared inventory to actual hidden/prepared window instances. discard_prewarm_window only mutates an in-memory set, so any already-created prepared window would remain alive and could still surface through later lifecycle flows, leaving VAL-LIFECYCLE-007 and VAL-CROSS-003 unproven in code.

issues.description: prewarm_window only records the enum in prepared_window_inventory and does not create, track, or hide any prepared backend window/resource. Because the feature contract is specifically about backend prewarm/discard commands and later open-window reuse, the implementation changes the API surface but not the actual window lifecycle behavior those commands are supposed to govern.

discard_prewarm_window removes an enum entry from the in-memory set, but it does not dispose of any potentially prepared window instance or other backend state. If a prewarmed hidden window existed, discard would leave that native state intact, so discarded prepared state could still participate in later enumeration/open flows. This falls short of the requirement that discarded prepared state must not be revived later.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker explicitly deviated from the skill tests-first step because no concrete prewarm inventory seam existed yet. The skill procedure may need to acknowledge that for backend contract redesigns, creating a minimal state seam before writing meaningful failing tests can be justified.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: worker-transcripts.jsonl entry for session 3c8b5e4e-b80b-4da3-bc3c-9f65cc892ff8 plus handoff.skillFeedback.deviations[0]

summary: Review failed: the typed command surface and tests are in place, but the implementation only tracks a synthetic enum inventory and does not actually prewarm/discard backend window state, so discard/non-resurrection guarantees are not fully implemented.
